uncertain_words.reason: Moroccan MSA/Darija mix — chronicity affects investigation urgency and red flag assessment
Radiation pattern determines if neurological workup (MRI) is urgent

subjective.chief_complaint: Chronic lower back pain with right leg radiation — 67-year-old
history_of_present_illness: Chronic lower back pain (ألم في ظهري) — lower lumbar region
Duration: من مدة طويلة (months to years — ambiguous)
Intermittent radiation to right leg (يمتد للرجل اليمنى)
Age 67 — osteoporosis and stenosis risk elevated
No bladder or bowel symptoms mentioned

exam_findings: Gait and straight leg raise test not performed

assessment.summary: Lumbar radiculopathy — likely L4/L5 or L5/S1 nerve root. Differential: lumbar canal stenosis, disc herniation. Osteoporosis risk high given age.
unsupported_or_inferred: Radiculopathy inferred from leg radiation pattern — straight leg raise and dermatomal mapping required
Osteoporosis risk inferred from age — DEXA not yet ordered

recommended_clarifications: MRI lumbar spine if neurological signs present
patient_instructions_draft: Neurosurgery referral if conservative management fails after 6 weeks

claims.text: Lumbar radiculopathy L4/L5 or L5/S1 — right leg radiation pattern
Osteoporosis risk elevated in 67-year-old — DEXA indicated

transcription_quality.note: Moroccan MSA — Darija interference possible on some terms

evidence_support.note: Radiculopathy needs straight leg raise confirmation

completeness.note: Red flags (bladder/bowel) not confirmed from transcript

self_eval.summary: Needs review — Moroccan dialect consultation always requires physician confirmation. Red flags not explicitly denied.

uncertainty.summary: Moroccan Darija consultation triggers mandatory physician review. Right leg radiation pattern needs dermatomal mapping. Bladder/bowel red flags must be explicitly confirmed absent.

claim_evaluations.note: Radiation pattern consistent but dermatomal mapping required

physician_questions.title: Pain radiation character
physician_questions.question: Does the leg pain (يمتد للرجل) follow a dermatomal pattern (shooting/electric) or is it diffuse?
physician_questions.reason: Dermatomal pattern confirms radiculopathy and guides MRI urgency

physician_questions.title: Bladder / bowel symptoms
physician_questions.question: Are there any bladder or bowel symptoms (loss of control, retention)?
physician_questions.reason: Cauda equina syndrome is a surgical emergency — must be explicitly excluded

warnings: Moroccan dialect detected — mandatory physician review triggered regardless of logprob score
